_note: Worked example: Bluestone Mutual Bank, a fictional regional retail/commercial bank. Four source systems (CoreVault Banking, PayStream Hub, CardLink Processor, Sentinel AML) feed a raw->stg->xref->core derived flow, a payment lifecycle state machine, an AML alert funnel, a GL daily summary, a window-sum balance roll-forward, and a transactions-vs-GL reconciliation. Keys starting with '_' are comments stripped before validation.

calendar._note: Card-dominated consumer shape: near-flat week with a Fri/Sat lift (card auths peak Friday-Saturday). The weekend dip on ACH/wire comes from business-day posting logic in the core.posted_transaction derivation, not from the calendar — both channels draw from the same calendar but only payments get posted on business days.

tables.name: application
tables.purpose: Application catalog for the fictional landscape.

tables.name: branch
tables.purpose: Branch network.

columns.name: branch_name
columns._note: city is drawn first so branch names stay coherent with their place.

tables.name: product
tables.purpose: Deposit, CD, and loan product catalog.

columns.name: product_type
columns._note: Drives fk affinity matching from corebank.account.account_type.

tables._note: Customer master. created_at is sorted with 72% backfill so customer IDs correlate with onboarding order and the book is tenured (in-window volume grows ~5%/yr instead of ramping from zero).
tables.name: customer
tables.purpose: Core banking customer master (retail, small business, commercial).

columns.name: customer_name
columns._note: Retail customers are persons, businesses get company names — one global generator here is a tell.

tables._note: Accounts open within ~a year of the customer relationship via date_offset from parent.created_at — long activity windows must NOT use date_offset, but onboarding lags are exactly what it is for.
tables.name: account
tables.purpose: Deposit, CD, and loan accounts.

columns.name: product_id
columns._note: Affinity match on product_type with a 2% leak — the leak feeds DQ-001 (account/product type mismatch).

columns.name: activity_tier
columns._note: Volume lever for per_parent scale_by: accounts onboarded late in the horizon get proportionally less activity, so per-parent draws don't dump a full two-year volume into the final weeks (the classic as_of pile-up artifact). parent_copy values arrive ISO-serialized, so plain string comparison buckets the date.

gen.expression: status if customer_since < '2025-04-01' else ('onboarding' if customer_since >= '2025-10-01' else 'ramping')

tables.name: merchant
tables.purpose: Card-accepting merchants seen by the processor.

tables._note: Cards exist mostly on checking accounts (scale_by on account_type); card_status is conditioned on the parent account status so closed accounts carry closed cards.
tables.name: card
tables.purpose: Debit cards issued against deposit accounts.

columns.name: usage_tier
columns._note: Same vintage-tier trick as corebank.account.activity_tier: cards on recently opened accounts spend at a ramp rate, not a two-year rate.

gen.expression: card_status if account_opened_date < '2025-04-01' else ('new_issue' if account_opened_date >= '2025-10-01' else 'ramping')

tables._note: The big card fact. txn_date is a calendar-weighted date bounded by card activation; auth_ts is derived from it via auto_created_at so date and timestamp agree. Volume conditioned on card_status plus a heavy-tailed per-card multiplier (spender whales).
tables.name: card_transaction
tables.purpose: Card authorizations from the processor; approved ones post to accounts.
tables.grain: one row per card authorization attempt

columns.name: merchant_id
columns._note: zipf 1.1: genuinely hot merchants — top 1% of merchants carry ~35-40% of card volume, matching the industry reference.

columns.name: amount
columns._note: price_endings give retail price points (x.99/x.49) — real card amount columns repeat exact values.

tables._note: ACH and wire instructions. Amount realism: lognormal draw quantized by a recurring-payment rounding unit via an expression — recurring payroll/bill amounts repeat exactly (duplicate mass), wires snap to 100/1000. Payment-type mix is conditioned on segment so commercial customers dominate wire value.
tables.name: payment_instruction
tables.purpose: ACH and wire payment instructions flowing through the payment hub.

columns.name: rounding_unit
columns._note: Recurring-payment quantum: 0.01 = exact-cents one-offs; 5/25/100/1000 = recurring payroll, bills, and round wires.

columns.name: wire_scale
columns._note: Wires are commercial-dominated in VALUE: retail wires are small one-offs, commercial wires are large. Applied only to wires in the amount expression so ACH payroll/bill amounts stay segment-neutral.

columns.name: amount
columns.gen: expr:round(round((amount_raw * (wire_scale if is_wire == 1 else 1)) / rounding_unit) * rounding_unit, 2)

tables.name: payment_event
tables.purpose: Payment lifecycle history emitted by the state machine (CDC-style).
tables.grain: one row per instruction per state entered

tables.name: monitoring_rule
tables.purpose: AML transaction monitoring rule catalog.

tables._note: Alerts fire on a small share of payment instructions (~1.5%), skewed toward wires via scale_by — generated next morning by the overnight batch (date_offset +1 day from initiation).
tables.name: alert
tables.purpose: AML monitoring alerts feeding the triage/case/SAR funnel.

tables.name: alert_event
tables.purpose: Alert review history emitted by the state machine.
tables.grain: one row per alert per state entered

tables.name: gl_account
tables.purpose: Chart of accounts: clearing/settlement accounts that tie to transactions plus income/expense accounts.

tables.name: file_load
tables.purpose: Settlement and statement file load log (batch exchange with networks).

tables.name: rule
tables.purpose: Data quality rule catalog (results come from the dq_rule_result_current view).

cases.2: customer missing Sentinel AML party mapping
cases.5: expired identifier mapping still flagged active
cases.6: payment lifecycle events out of sequence

tables.name: recon_control
tables.purpose: Reconciliation control register; break details come from the control views.

cases.1: Posted transactions to GL deposits movement
cases.3: Card settlement file to posted card volume
cases.4: AML alert to case linkage completeness

tables._note: Everything below is populated by SQL derivations — real lineage from the source tables above.
tables.name: corebank_customer_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly fixed-width core extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: corebank_customer
tables.purpose: Cleaned core customers; normalizes raw extract dates and casing.

tables.name: entity_identifier
tables.purpose: Identifier crosswalk: CoreVault customer IDs and Sentinel AML party references for canonical customers.

tables.name: customer
tables.purpose: Canonical customer joining staging and the crosswalk.

tables.name: account
tables.purpose: Canonical account enriched with product attributes.

tables._note: The posting ledger: approved card transactions, posted ACH/wire payments (business-day adjusted), and payment returns. The restatement_reversal imperfection lands here post-derivation, creating the transactions-vs-GL recon breaks.
tables.name: posted_transaction
tables.purpose: Canonical posted transactions across card, ACH, and wire channels.
tables.grain: one row per posted transaction, return, or reversal on an account

tables.name: gl_daily_summary
tables.purpose: Daily GL movement by account: double-entry summary derived from posted transactions (deposits GL vs channel clearing GLs).
tables.grain: one row per gl_code per business date with activity

tables.name: case_file
tables.purpose: Investigation cases opened from escalated alerts (every case references an alert by construction).

tables.name: dim_date
tables.purpose: Calendar dimension.

tables.name: dim_customer
tables.purpose: Customer dimension from the canonical layer.

tables.name: dim_account
tables.purpose: Account dimension.

tables.name: dim_merchant
tables.purpose: Merchant dimension.

tables.name: fact_transaction
tables.purpose: Posted transaction fact.

tables._note: Balance roll-forward derived via SQL window sums over the posting ledger — opening + credits - debits = closing holds per account per day by construction, with a deterministic per-account opening anchor.
tables.name: fact_account_balance
tables.purpose: Daily account balance roll-forward.
tables.grain: one row per account per posting date with activity (sparse roll-forward)

state_machines._note: Payment lifecycle: initiated -> screened -> posted | returned, with a screening-hold detour and right-censoring so instructions near the as_of date legitimately sit mid-pipeline.
state_machines.name: payment_lifecycle

state_machines._note: AML funnel: ~87% of triaged alerts close as false positives; escalations become cases (derived aml.case_file); ~15% of investigations file a SAR. Both terminal closes stamp the same closed_at column.
state_machines.name: aml_alert_review

derivations.name: raw_corebank_customer_extract
derivations._note: Raw landing copy with US-format dates; the format_drift imperfection later pads a slice of these, which DQ-003 catches at query time.
sql: select cast(customer_id as text), customer_no, customer_name, segment, kyc_status, city, state,

derivations.name: stg_corebank_customer
sql: select cast(customer_id as integer), customer_no, trim(customer_name), lower(segment), kyc_status, city, state,

derivations.name: xref_entity_identifier
derivations._note: Two source systems per canonical customer: the CoreVault customer ID and the Sentinel AML party reference. missing_xref and stale_mapping imperfections degrade this table post-derivation.
sql: select row_number() over (order by customer_id), 'customer', customer_id,
'CoreVault Banking', 'customer', cast(customer_id as text), date(created_at), null, 1
select 500000 + row_number() over (order by customer_id), 'customer', customer_id,

derivations.name: core_customer
sql: select s.customer_id, s.customer_no, s.customer_name, s.segment, s.kyc_status, s.city, s.state, s.onboarded_date,
where x.canonical_entity_id = s.customer_id and x.source_system = 'Sentinel AML'

derivations.name: core_account
sql: select a.account_id, a.account_no, a.iban, a.customer_id, a.segment, a.account_type,
left join corebank.product p on p.product_id = a.product_id;

derivations.name: core_posted_transaction
derivations._note: Card auths post on transaction date (consumer spend runs 7 days a week); ACH/wire post on the next business day — that derivation step is what creates the weekend dip on human channels while card volume stays flat. Returns reverse the original with direction flipped and the original amount (return amount = original amount invariant).
sql: left join cardlink.merchant m on m.merchant_id = c.merchant_id
case when p.payment_type like 'wire%' then 'wire' else 'ach' end,
case when p.payment_type like 'wire%' then 'wire' else 'ach' end,
case p.direction when 'debit' then 'credit' else 'debit' end,
p.amount, p.counterparty_name, 'returned: insufficient funds or closed account'

derivations.name: ledger_gl_daily_summary
derivations._note: Double-entry by construction: every transaction moves the customer deposits GL (2010) against its channel clearing GL (1310/1320/1330), so total debits = total credits per business date.
sql: select row_number() over (order by business_date, gl_code), business_date, gl_code,
select t.txn_date as business_date, '2010' as gl_code,
case when t.direction = 'debit' then t.amount else 0 end as debit_amount,
case when t.direction = 'credit' then t.amount else 0 end as credit_amount
case t.channel when 'card' then '1310' when 'ach' then '1320' else '1330' end,
case when t.direction = 'credit' then t.amount else 0 end,
case when t.direction = 'debit' then t.amount else 0 end

derivations.name: aml_case_file
sql: select a.alert_id, 'CASE-' || printf('%05d', a.alert_id), a.alert_id, a.account_id,

derivations.name: wh_dim_date
sql: union all select date(dt, '+1 day') from d where dt < '2025-12-31'
select dt, cast(strftime('%Y', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%m', dt) as integer),
cast(strftime('%d', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%w', dt) as integer),
case when strftime('%w', dt) in ('0', '6') then 1 else 0 end

derivations.name: wh_dim_customer
sql: select customer_uid, customer_no, customer_name, segment, kyc_status, city, state from core.customer;

derivations.name: wh_dim_account
sql: select account_uid, account_no, account_type, product_code, segment, status from core.account;

derivations.name: wh_dim_merchant
sql: select merchant_id, merchant_ref, merchant_name, mcc, city, state from cardlink.merchant;

derivations.name: wh_fact_transaction
sql: select t.txn_id, t.account_id, a.customer_id, t.txn_date, t.channel, t.txn_type,
join corebank.account a on a.account_id = t.account_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_account_balance
derivations._note: Window-sum roll-forward: opening + credits - debits = closing per account per posting day, anchored to a deterministic per-account opening balance (no random()).
sql: round(sum(case when direction = 'credit' then amount else 0 end), 2) as credits,
round(sum(case when direction = 'debit' then amount else 0 end), 2) as debits
sum(credits - debits) over (partition by account_id order by txn_date
rows between unbounded preceding and current row) as run_net
select row_number() over (order by account_id, txn_date),

derivations.name: mart_txn_channel_daily
sql: select txn_date, channel, count(*) as txn_count,

derivations.name: mart_aml_funnel
sql: sum(case when c.case_id is not null then 1 else 0 end) as linked_cases
left join aml.case_file c on c.alert_id = a.alert_id

derivations.name: control_recon_txn_vs_gl
derivations._note: Reconciliation as a VIEW so it reflects post-derivation imperfections: the GL summary was built before the restatement_reversal pairs landed in core.posted_transaction, so the affected dates break.
sql: round(t.txn_debits, 2) as txn_debits, round(t.txn_credits, 2) as txn_credits,
round(g.total_debit, 2) as gl_debits, round(g.total_credit, 2) as gl_credits,
sum(case when direction = 'debit' then amount else 0 end) as txn_debits,
sum(case when direction = 'credit' then amount else 0 end) as txn_credits
from core.posted_transaction group by txn_date) t

derivations.name: dq_rule_result_current
sql: select 'DQ-001' as rule_code, 'account product type mismatch' as issue, cast(account_uid as text) as entity_id
from core.account where product_type is not null and account_type <> product_type
select 'DQ-002', 'customer missing Sentinel party mapping', cast(c.customer_id as text)
where not exists (select 1 from xref.entity_identifier x
from raw.corebank_customer_extract where source_created_date not like '__/__/____'
select 'DQ-004', 'card transaction references unknown merchant', cast(c.card_txn_id as text)
and not exists (select 1 from cardlink.merchant m where m.merchant_id = c.merchant_id)
select 'DQ-005', 'expired identifier mapping still flagged active', cast(x.entity_identifier_id as text)
where x.valid_to is not null and x.valid_to < '2025-12-31' and x.active_flag = 1
select 'DQ-006', 'payment lifecycle events out of sequence', cast(e1.instruction_id as text)
on e2.instruction_id = e1.instruction_id and e2.event_seq = e1.event_seq + 1
select 'DQ-007', 'verified customer missing contact email', cast(customer_id as text)
from corebank.customer where kyc_status = 'verified' and (email is null or email = '');

imperfections.name: customer_name_typos
imperfections.scenario: Branch staff hand-key names at onboarding; typos accumulate and feed the MDM match queue.

imperfections.name: dup_customer_onboarding
imperfections.scenario: Same person onboarded twice via branch and mobile channels — split customer view and duplicate AML alerting downstream.

imperfections.name: customer_email_nulls
imperfections.scenario: Contact email never captured for a slice of the book; DQ-007 counts the gap.

imperfections.name: settlement_late_arrival
imperfections.scenario: Card settlement files arrive T+2 instead of T+1 — recon timing noise on daily card volume.

imperfections.name: ghost_merchants
imperfections.scenario: Merchant IDs missing from the processor's merchant master; DQ-004 catches them and the posting ledger shows UNKNOWN MERCHANT.

imperfections.name: payment_event_disorder
imperfections.scenario: Payment hub CDC events land out of sequence; DQ-006 flags status regressions.

imperfections.name: duplicate_file_loads
imperfections.scenario: Scheduler retries log duplicate settlement file loads.

imperfections.name: alert_disposition_overrides
imperfections.scenario: Analysts override model dispositions with supervisor sign-off; visible in mart_aml_funnel.

imperfections.name: missing_party_xref
imperfections.scenario: Mappings lost in the Sentinel AML re-platforming; DQ-002 reports customers without an active party mapping.

imperfections.name: stale_party_xref
imperfections.scenario: Expired identifier mappings never re-pointed after the core conversion; DQ-005 catches active rows with past valid_to.

imperfections.name: raw_extract_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Legacy fixed-width extract job pads dates after a patch; DQ-003 counts unparseable rows.

imperfections.name: gl_restatements
imperfections.scenario: Manual fee reversals posted after the GL summary ran — these create the control_recon_txn_vs_gl breaks.

controls.rule_name: Posted transactions tie to GL deposits movement
Every AML case references an alert

dq_rules.condition: every customer has an active Sentinel AML party mapping
active mappings have no past valid_to

dataflows.name: customer_mastering
dataflows.sequence: corebank.customer -> raw extract -> staging -> xref -> core.customer -> wh.dim_customer

dataflows.name: payment_lifecycle
dataflows.sequence: paystream.payment_instruction -> state machine -> paystream.payment_event -> core.posted_transaction (business-day posting) -> wh.fact_transaction

dataflows.name: balance_rollforward
dataflows.sequence: core.posted_transaction -> window sums -> wh.fact_account_balance (opening + credits - debits = closing)

dataflows.name: aml_funnel
dataflows.sequence: paystream.payment_instruction -> aml.alert -> state machine -> aml.case_file -> SAR filings
